name: Agent Delegation
description: Let the main agent delegate tasks to specialist agents

function.name: ask_agent
function.description: Delegate a task to a specialist agent. Chain multiple calls when needed.

agent_id.description: The ID of the specialist agent to delegate to.

task.description: The exact task or question to give the specialist. Be specific and complete — include any relevant context like date ranges, amounts, or recipients. When asking an email agent to send a file from shared-docs, include the doc_id in the task and tell the agent to pass it as attachment_doc_ids to email_compose.

background.description: STRONGLY RECOMMENDED — set to TRUE for any task that will take more than a few seconds: skill creation, code refactoring, multi-step file edits, research, configuration changes, deployments, ANYTHING where the specialist will call multiple tools or write code. Setting true returns IMMEDIATELY with a 'started' message — the user can keep chatting with you while the specialist works in the background, and a live task chip appears in chat showing progress. When the specialist finishes, their result lands as a follow-up message you can read on your next turn. ONLY use background:false (synchronous, BLOCKS the chat until the specialist responds) for quick Q&A where the answer feeds directly into your next sentence: looking up a single value, checking a single fact, simple one-tool-call lookups. If in doubt, set true. Synchronous mode is the wrong default — it locks the user out of chatting with you for the full duration of the specialist's work.

no_confirm.description: If true, instruct the specialist to skip its 'show draft and wait for approval' gate for this one delegation. Use when the user has explicitly authorized the action in the message that triggered this delegation (e.g. 'just send it', 'no need to confirm'). Defaults to false.
